comments:
Service Worker Schema — Pure data types for offline-first capabilities.

Models the Service Worker lifecycle as algebraic data types.
Service Workers enable offline functionality, caching, and push notifications.

## Design Philosophy

Service Workers at billion-agent scale require:
- Explicit lifecycle states (no hidden state transitions)
- Deterministic cache strategies
- Type-safe update handling
- Clear registration state

## Service Worker Lifecycle

```
  ┌────────────┐
  │ Installing │──────► error ──► Redundant
  └─────┬──────┘
        │ installed
        ▼
  ┌───────────┐
  │ Installed │ (waiting)
  └─────┬─────┘
        │ activating
        ▼
  ┌────────────┐
  │ Activating │──────► error ──► Redundant
  └─────┬──────┘
        │ activated
        ▼
  ┌───────────┐
  │ Activated │ (controlling)
  └─────┬─────┘
        │ update replaces
        ▼
  ┌───────────┐
  │ Redundant │
  └───────────┘
```

## Usage

```purescript
import Hydrogen.Schema.Network.ServiceWorker as SW

-- Register a service worker
config = SW.config (SW.scriptURL "/sw.js")
  { scope = Just (SW.scope "/app/")
  , updateViaCache = SW.Imports
  }

-- Handle lifecycle events
case event of
  SW.SWInstalling -> showInstalling
  SW.SWActivated -> showReady
  SW.SWRedundant -> showUpdateAvailable
```

declarations.comments:
Service Worker lifecycle state.

Represents the complete lifecycle from installation to redundancy.
declarations.title: ServiceWorkerState

declarations.comments: Is the worker installed and waiting?
declarations.title: isInstalled

declarations.comments: Is the worker active and controlling?
declarations.title: isActivated

declarations.comments: Has the worker been replaced or failed?
declarations.title: isRedundant

declarations.comments: Is the worker in an active state (activating or activated)?
declarations.title: isActive

declarations.comments: Is the worker waiting to become active?
declarations.title: isWaiting

declarations.comments:
Service Worker script URL.

Path to the JavaScript file containing the worker code.
declarations.title: ScriptURL

declarations.comments:
Service Worker scope.

Determines which URLs the worker controls.
Default is the directory containing the script.
declarations.title: Scope

declarations.comments: How to handle HTTP cache for service worker updates.
declarations.title: UpdateViaCache

declarations.comments: Create a configuration with script URL.
declarations.title: config

declarations.comments: Information about a specific Service Worker.
declarations.title: WorkerInfo

declarations.comments:
Caching strategies for Service Worker.

These strategies determine how the worker handles fetch requests.
declarations.title: CacheStrategy

declarations.comments: Should the page refresh to get new version?
declarations.title: needsRefresh

declarations.comments:
Navigation preload state.

Navigation preload allows starting the network request for a
navigation in parallel with service worker startup.
declarations.title: NavigationPreload

declarations.comments:
Complete Service Worker state.

Combines registration, update, and capability states.
declarations.title: SWConnectionState

declarations.comments: State when SW is controlling the page.
declarations.title: controlledSWState

name: Hydrogen.Schema.Network.ServiceWorker
